name: barbell side split squat v. 2
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outwards.
Hold a barbell across your upper back, resting it on your shoulders.
Take a big step to the side with your right foot, keeping your left foot planted.
Bend your right knee and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your chest up and your back straight.
Push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side, stepping out with your left foot.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outwards. Hold a barbell across your upper back, resting it on your shoulders. Take a big step to the side with your right foot, keeping your left foot planted. Bend your right knee and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your chest up and your back straight. Push through your right heel to return to the starting position. Repeat on the other side, stepping out with your left foot. Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Axial loading under fatigue may worsen asymmetrical curves. Prefer split-stance or supported variations.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Loaded spinal flexion or heavy axial loading should be avoided. Use supported variations.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
